content.value: Thank you for signing in. Soon you will be able to handle notification preferences, save your favourite teams and competitions against your profile and synchronise across your devices. If you have questions on how FIFA uses your personal data please visit

breakingNewsProps.message: Get ready for the biggest ever FIFA World Cup™ with FIFA.com

rankingListLabels.unrankedDescriptionLabel: Teams who have not played a match in the last 48 months will be unranked.
rankingListLabels.matchWindowResultsTooltipLabel: Results since previous version of the World Ranking
rankingListLabels.matchWindowResultAdditionalDescription: Matches date are displayed in local timezone of the matches
rankingListLabels.liveRankingDisclaimerLabel: The ranking is considered unofficial until all matches are approved as international 'A' matches. [Last official ranking from 1 April](https://inside.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/men?dateId=FRS_Male_Football_20260119)
rankingListLabels.unofficialMatchDisclaimer: *Some matches are happening outside live windows and the new official ranking is calculated at the end of the next live window.

content.value: The ranking is considered unofficial until all matches are approved as international 'A' matches and points are verified.
If a match is downgraded below A-international status after it occurs, any points awarded from the match will not be counted in the next official ranking update
When matches occur outside of official international match windows, rankings will not be updated until the end of the next ranking window
For certain matches where full coverage is not available, live rankings will only be updated at full time

The FIFA/Coca-Cola World Ranking is determined using the Elo Model. This method adds or subtracts points for matches to or from a team's existing point total. The points which are added or subtracted are partially determined by:

target.title: More on how the Men's Ranking is calculated

content.value: More on how the Men's Ranking is calculated

videoLabels.errorMsg: This stream is currently unavailable. Please try to refresh the page.

content.articleTitle: Gibraltar: a decade of hard work beginning to bear fruit
content.description: Taking things step by step is often essential to achieving long-term progress and avoiding unnecessary setbacks. That approach perfectly sums up the first 10 years of the Gibraltar Football Association (GFA) as a FIFA Member Association (MA).

image.title: Roy Chipolina celebrates a goal with the Gibraltar national team
image.alt: Roy Chipolina celebrates a goal with the Gibraltar national team

content.articleTitle: Kosovo: Ten years of growth on the global stage
content.description: On 13 May 2026, Kosovo celebrate their 10th year as a FIFA Member Association (MA). A decade on, Kosovo’s footballing growth has been remarkable, reflected in consistent progress in both the men’s and women’s FIFA/Coca-Cola World Rankings.

image.title: Dion Gallapeni of Kosovo celebrates with teammates
image.alt: BRATISLAVA, SLOVAKIA - MARCH 26: Dion Gallapeni of Kosovo celebrates after winning the FIFA World Cup 2026 European Qualifiers KO play-offs match between Slovakia and Kosovo at Narodny Futbalovy Stadion on March 26, 2026 in Bratislava, Slovakia. (Photo by Matej Sagan/Eurasia Sport Images/Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: France reclaim top spot in the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking
content.description: The first period in which the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking has been updated in real time during national team matches has brought a raft of changes to the standings with a total of 166 men’s internationals have been played since the last update

image.title: Désiré Doué of France celebrates scoring
image.alt: LANDOVER, MARYLAND - MARCH 29: Desire Doue of France celebrates scoring his team's third goal during the international friendly match between Colombia and France at Northwest Stadium on March 29, 2026 in Landover, Maryland. (Photo by Hannah Foslien/Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: FIFA/Coca-Cola World Rankings go live with real-time updates during men’s and women’s national team matches
content.description: The FIFA/Coca-Cola World Rankings will now be updated live in an exciting and innovative change that will mean fans can see where their country ranks as goals are scored in matches around the world.

content.articleTitle: AFCON finalists Morocco and Senegal steal limelight in January 2026 FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking
content.description: The recently concluded CAF Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) has made a significant impact on the January 2026 edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking, with both finalists, perhaps unsurprisingly, making significant strides.

image.title: Ismaila Sarr of Senegal and Abdessmad Ezzalzouli of Morocco battle for the ball during the AFCON final between Morocco and Senegal at Complexe Sportif Prince Moulay Abdellah, Rabat
image.alt: Ismaila Sarr of Senegal and Abdessmad Ezzalzouli of Morocco battle for the ball during the AFCON final between Morocco and Senegal at Complexe Sportif Prince Moulay Abdellah, Rabat, Morocco on January 18, 2026. (Photo by Ulrik Pedersen/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: Spain end 2025 atop FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking, Jordan jump
content.description: The December 2025 edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking – the last of the year – has landed following the latest 41 international duels, with the FIFA Arab Cup Qatar 2025™having taken centre stage.

image.title: Mikel Merino of Spain celebrates
image.alt: Mikel Merino of Spain celebrates a goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifier match between Spain and Bulgaria at Jose Zorrilla Stadium in Valladolid, Spain, on October 14. (Photo by Guille Martinez/f22photo/NurPhoto via Getty Images) (Photo by Guillermo Martinez/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: Spain first as Brazil bolster top 10 berth in FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking
content.description: With the latest round of qualifiers done and dusted, UEFA and Concacaf sides punched their tickets to the FIFA World Cup 2026™ and claimed the final direct spots ahead of the Play-Off Tournament and European Play-Off draws to be held on 20 November.

image.title: Estevao of Brazil celebrates after scoring his team's first goal with teammates during the international friendly match between Brazil and Tunisia in Lille, France
image.alt: LILLE, FRANCE - NOVEMBER 18: Estevao of Brazil celebrates after scoring his team's first goal with teammates during the international friendly match between Brazil and Tunisia at Decathlon Arena on November 18, 2025 in Lille, France

content.articleTitle: Argentina and Germany headline top 10 reshuffle in latest FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking
content.description: Following a thrilling round of 173 national-team outings that included FIFA World Cup 26™ qualifiers across five continents, and a flurry of friendlies, the October edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking serves up several eye-catching changes.

image.title: Nick Woltemade of Germany celebrates after scoring his side's first goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifier match between Northern Ireland and Germany at the National Football Stadium at Windsor Park in Belfast
image.alt: 13 October 2025; Nick Woltemade of Germany celebrates after scoring his side's first goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifier match between Northern Ireland and Germany at the National Football Stadium at Windsor Park in Belfast. (Photo By Ramsey Cardy/Sportsfile via Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: Spain surge back to first place in FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking amid top 10 shake-up
content.description: After a hiatus following the rousing success of the inaugural FIFA Club World Cup™, men’s international football action returned to the global stage with a bang earlier this month in the shape of a string of FIFA World Cup 26™ qualifiers

image.title: Spain's #21 Mikel Oiarzabal celebrates with #19 Lamine Yamal after scoring during the FIFA World Cup 2026 European qualifying match against Bulgaria on September 4, 2025
image.alt: Spain's #21 Mikel Oiarzabal celebrates with #19 Lamine Yamal after scoring during the FIFA World Cup 2026 European qualifying match against Bulgaria on September 4, 2025. (Photo by NIKOLAY DOYCHINOV/AFP via Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: Croatia back in Top 10 of FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking; Mexico make waves
content.description: World champions Argentina remain out ahead in the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking, while Mexico have the top 10 in sight after claiming Concacaf Gold Cup glory.

image.title: Mexico's forward #09 Raul Jimenez celebrates
image.alt: Mexico's forward #09 Raul Jimenez celebrates scoring his team's first goal during the Concacaf Gold Cup final football match between Mexico and USA at NRG Stadium in Houston, Texas on July 6, 2025. (Photo by Chandan Khanna / AFP) (Photo by CHANDAN KHANNA/AFP via Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: Back in the Top 50 and keeping their World Cup dreams alive, Paraguay's development efforts pay off
content.description: Paraguay last featured in the FIFA/Coca-Cola World Ranking Top 50 in July 2023. They also cemented their ambition to return to the World Cup for the first time since 2010.  APF Head of Development speaks about this.

image.title: Image > Players of Paraguay pose for a team photo prior to the FIFA World Cup 2026 Qualifiers match vs Chile
image.alt: ASUNCION, PARAGUAY - MARCH 20: Players of Paraguay pose for a team photo prior to the FIFA World Cup 2026 Qualifiers match between Paraguay and Chile at Estadio Defensores del Chaco on March 20, 2025 in Asuncion, Paraguay. (Photo by Eduardo Velázquez/Eurasia Sport Images/Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: Spain seize second spot in latest FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking™
content.description: Argentina (1st) remain top thanks to impressive wins away to Uruguay (1-0) and at home to Brazil (4-1), but the Albiceleste have new company, as Spain (2nd, up 1) took full advantage of France’s (3rd, down 1) slip-up in Croatia to secure second spot.

image.title: Spain's players celebrate
image.alt: Spain's players celebrate after the UEFA Nations League quarter final second leg football match between Spain and Netherlands at the Mestalla stadium in Valencia, on March 23, 2025. (Photo by JOSE JORDAN / AFP) (Photo by JOSE JORDAN/AFP via Getty Images)

content.articleTitle: Annual FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking™ glee for Argentina and Angola
content.description: Just as they did in 2023, Argentina (1st) sign off for the year in top spot, with France (2nd) and Spain (3rd) joining them on the podium.

image.title: Zini of Angola celebrates
image.alt: Zini of Angola celebrates goal during the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations Qualifiers match between Angola and Ghana at Estadio 11 de Novembro in Luanda, Angola on 16 November 2024 ©Ampe Rogerio/BackpagePix

content.articleTitle: Nip and tuck at the top while Niger hit the heights
content.description: This month’s international action featured no fewer than 192 encounters providing a major impact across the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men’s World Ranking where Canada, Kosovo and Comoros reached new heights while Niger were the biggest movers.

image.title: Oumar Sako of Niger (L) celebrates with teammate Boubacar Djibrill Goumey
image.alt: Oumar Sako of Niger (L) celebrates goal with teammate Boubacar Djibrill Goumey during the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations Qualifiers match between Niger and Ghana at Stade Municipal de Berkane in Berkane, Morocco on 9 September 2024 ©Nabil Ramdani/BackpagePix

content.articleTitle: FIFA Support Helps Botswana, Comoros and Angola climb the FIFA/Coca-Cola World Ranking
content.description: October witnessed outstanding performances from three African teams in the qualifiers for the next CAF Africa Cup of Nations, which will take place in Morocco from December 21 2025, to January 12 2026.

image.title: Botswana's players pose for a team photo
image.alt: Botswana's players pose for a team photo during the 2025 CAF Africa Cup of Nations (CAN) group C football match between Botswana and Egypt at Francistown Stadium in Francistown on September 10, 2024. (Photo by Monirul Bhuiyan / AFP) (Photo by MONIRUL BHUIYAN/AFP via Getty Images)

(Photo by Quinn Rooney/Getty Images)
asset.caption: SYDNEY, AUSTRALIA - AUGUST 20: Mariona Caldentey of Spain and Georgia Stanway of England compete for the ball  during the FIFA Women's World Cup Australia & New Zealand 2023 Final match between Spain and England at Stadium Australia on August 20, 2023 in Sydney, Australia. cards.title: FIFA/Coca-Cola Women's World Ranking Procedures
cards.description: In the FIFA Women's World Ranking (WWR) teams are ranked according to a value that is a measure of their actual strength. One of the benefits of this is that a considerable amount can be learned about the ranking value (or rating points) of a team.

asset.title: Magdalena Eriksson of Sweden and Sam Kerr of Australia compete for the ball
asset.alt: BRISBANE, AUSTRALIA - AUGUST 19: Magdalena Eriksson of Sweden and Sam Kerr of Australia compete for the ball during the FIFA Women's World Cup Australia & New Zealand 2023 Third Place Match match between Sweden and Australia at Brisbane Stadium on August 19, 2023 in Brisbane / Meaanjin, Australia. (Photo by Alex Pantling - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images)
asset.caption: BRISBANE, AUSTRALIA - AUGUST 19: Magdalena Eriksson of Sweden and Sam Kerr of Australia compete for the ball during the FIFA Women's World Cup Australia & New Zealand 2023 Third Place Match match between Sweden and Australia at Brisbane Stadium on August 19, 2023 in Brisbane / Meaanjin, Australia. (Photo by Alex Pantling - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images)
